sarga.title: Maricha's admonitions and warnings to Ravana

shlokas.original:
आज्ञप्तो राजवद्वाक्यं प्रतिकूलं निशाचरः।
अब्रवीत्परुषं वाक्यं मारीचो राक्षसाधिपम्।।3.41.1।।
shlokas.translation: At this royal command which was against his interest Maracha used harsh words to the demonking:

shlokas.original:
केनायमुपदिष्टस्ते विनाशः पापकर्मणा।
सपुत्रस्य सराष्ट्रस्य सामात्यस्य निशाचर।।3.41.2।।
shlokas.translation: Who is that sinner who has given you this advice which will spell destruction on you, your sons, your kingdom and your ministers ?

shlokas.original:
कस्त्वया सुखिना राजन्नाभिनन्दति पापकृत्।
केनेदमुपदिष्टं ते मृत्युद्वारमुपायतः।।3.41.3।।
shlokas.translation: Which sinner is not able to see you happy? O king by whom is this gate of death  shown to you cunningly?

shlokas.original:
शत्रवस्तव सुव्यक्तं हीनवीर्या निशाचराः।
इच्छन्ति त्वां विनश्यन्तमुपरुद्धं बलीयसा।।3.41.4।।
shlokas.translation: This is clear that your enemy demons are now powerless. Hence they want you to be engaged with a stronger power and be destroyed.

shlokas.original:
केनेदमुपदिष्टं ते क्षुद्रेणाहितवादिना।
यस्त्वामिच्छति नश्यन्तं स्वकृतेन निशाचर।।3.41.5।।
shlokas.translation: O Ravana by which mean fellow is this advice given against your welfare? Who wants you to be destroyed by your own action.

shlokas.original:
वध्याः खलु न हन्यन्ते सचिवास्तव रावण।
ये त्वामुत्पथमारूढं न निगृह्णन्ति सर्वशः।।3.41.6।।
shlokas.translation: O Ravana the ministers should have prevented you by all means from adopting this  wrong path. Since they have not done so, they deserve to be spain.

shlokas.original:
अमात्यैः कामवृत्तो हि राजा कापथमाश्रितः।
निग्राह्यस्सर्वथा सदभिर्न निग्राह्यो निगृह्यसे।।3.41.7।।
shlokas.translation: Good ministrers should always prevent a lustful king from taking resourse to an evil path. Why were you not stopped, even though you deserve to be checked.

shlokas.original:
धर्ममर्थं च कामं च यशश्च जयतां वर।
स्वामिप्रसादात्सचिवाः प्राप्नुवन्ति निशाचर।।3.41.8।।
shlokas.translation: O Ravana  the best among the successful ministers attain piety, prosperity, pleasures and fame by the grace of the king.

shlokas.original:
विपर्यये तु तत्सर्वं व्यर्थं भवति रावण।
व्यसनं स्वामिवैगुण्यात्प्राप्नुवन्तीतरे जनाः।।3.41.9।।
shlokas.translation: Otherwise evrything will become useless, O Ravana  Others will suffer calamities on account of their master's vices.

shlokas.original:
राजमूलो हि धर्मश्च जयश्च जयतां वर।
तस्मात्सर्वा���्ववस्थासु रक्षितव्या नराधिपाः।।3.41.10।।
shlokas.translation: O Ravana, the best among the victorious indeed the king is at the root of righteousness and victory.Therefore, the king should be protected by all at every stage.

shlokas.original:
राज्यं पालयितुं शक्यं न तीक्ष्णेन निशाचर।
न चापि प्रतिकूलेन नाविनीतेन राक्षस।।3.41.11।।
shlokas.translation: O nightwalking demon  a kingdom cannnot be governed by a king who is rude or hostile or impolite.

shlokas.original:
ये तीक्ष्णमन्त्रास्सचिवा भज्यन्ते सह तेन वै।
विषमे तुरगा श्शीघ्रा मन्दसारथयो यथा।।3.41.12।।
shlokas.translation: Ministers who adopt improper strategies go down along with the king just like swift horses driven by a slow charioteer on a rugged terrain.

shlokas.original:
बहवस्साधवो लोके युक्ता धर्ममनुष्ठिताः।
परेषामपराधेन विनष्टास्सपरिच्छदाः।।3.41.13।।
shlokas.translation: Persons who walk the righteous path go down in this world along with their kith and kin due to the mistakes committed by others .

shlokas.original:
स्वामिना प्रतिकूलेन प्रजास्तीक्ष्णेन रावण।
रक्ष्यमाणा न वर्धन्ते मेषा गोमायुना यथा।।3.41.14।।
shlokas.translation: O Ravana, people who are ruled by a cruel and hostile king will not grow like the sheep protected by a jackal.

shlokas.original:
अवश्यं विनशिष्यन्ति सर्वे रावण राक्षसाः।
येषां त्वं कर्कशो राजा दुर्बुद्धिरजितेन्द्रियः।।3.41.15।।
shlokas.translation: Under a rude, crooked king whose senses are not under his control the demons will surely perish.

shlokas.original:
तदिदं काकतालीयं घोरमासादितं मया।
अत्र किं शोचनीयस्त्वं ससैन्यो विनशिष्यसि।।3.41.16।।
shlokas.translation: I am faced with  this dreadful disaster accidentally. You will not only be in a miserable state because of your misdeeds, you will perish along with your army.

shlokas.original:
मां निहत्य तु रामश्च न चिरात्त्वां वधिष्यति।
अनेन कृतकृत्योऽस्मि म्रियेयमरिणा हतः।।3.41.17।।
shlokas.translation: Rama will kill you not long after killing me.Hence even if I die in the hands of the enemy, my desire will be fulfilled.

shlokas.original:
दर्शनादेव रामस्य हतं मामवधारय।
आत्मानं च हतं विद्धि हृत्वा सीतां सबान्धवम्।।3.41.18।।
shlokas.translation: Yu most understand that if I die at the very sight of Rama, you along with your relatives will perish with the abduction of Sita.

shlokas.original:
आनयिष्यसि चेत्सीतामाश्रमात्सहितो मया।
नैवत्वमसि नाहं च नैव लङ्का न राक्षसाः।।3.41.19।।
shlokas.translation: If you bring Sita from the hermitage with my assistance, know that none of us will surviveyou nor I, nor Lanka , nor the demons.

shlokas.original:
निवार्यमाणस्तु मया हितैषिणा न मृष्यसे वाक्यमिदं निशाचर।
परेतकल्पा हि गतायुषो नरा हितं न गृह्णन्ति सुहृद्भिरीरितम्।।3.41.20।।
shlokas.translation: O Ravana, you do not understand this word of warning coming from your wellwisher. Men who are going to die do not heed the advice given by their friends. इत्यार्ष�� श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे एकचत्वारिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fortyfirst sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
